---
title: "Heatmap Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# HeatmapWidget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Heatmap Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

## Completed Work Summary

The following features are complete and documented in [index.qmd](index.qmd):

- **Widget skeleton**: EditorState pattern, registration, state/view/properties factories
- **State management**: `HeatmapState` with `PlotAlignmentState`, `RelativeTimeAxisState`, `VerticalAxisState` composition
- **Axis system**: `RelativeTimeAxisWidget` (X) and `VerticalAxisWidget` (Y) with bidirectional sync
- **Interaction**: Separated X/Y zoom, pan, double-click time navigation via shared `PlotInteractionHelpers`
- **Properties panel**: Alignment controls (`PlotAlignmentWidget`), axis range controls in collapsible sections
- **OpenGL lifecycle**: `SceneRenderer` initialization, background color, projection/view matrix management
- **State serialization**: Full round-trip JSON via `rfl::json`
- **Unit selection state** (Phase 1.1): `HeatmapStateData::unit_keys` with full add/remove/batch API and `unitsChanged()` signal
- **Unit selection UI** (Phase 1.2): `Feature_Tree_Widget` embedded in properties panel, filtered to `DigitalEventSeries`, with prefix-based group toggling

**Not yet implemented**: Data gathering, rate estimation, colormap rendering, sorting, and all cross-widget linking described below.

---

## Phase 1: Core Rendering — Per-Unit Rate Matrix

**Status**: Phases 1.1 and 1.2 complete. Phases 1.3–1.5 not started; rate computation is a
direct reuse of the [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) binning pipeline.

**Goal**: Produce a `units × time_bins` firing rate matrix and render it as a colored heatmap.

### 1.1 Unit Selection in State ✅

`HeatmapStateData` stores the selected units as a flat key list (serialized via `rfl::json`):

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    std::vector<std::string> unit_keys;  ///< Selected DigitalEventSeries keys
};
```

`HeatmapState` exposes a full management API:

```cpp
void addUnit(std::string const & key);
void removeUnit(std::string const & key);
void addUnits(std::vector<std::string> const & keys);    // batch
void removeUnits(std::vector<std::string> const & keys); // batch
[[nodiscard]] bool hasUnit(std::string const & key) const;
[[nodiscard]] std::vector<std::string> const & unitKeys() const;
```

All mutating methods emit `unitsChanged()` (for UI sync) and `stateChanged()` (for scene
rebuild). Addition is idempotent — duplicate keys are silently ignored.

Note: `HeatmapUnitConfig` (with a separate `display_label`) was simplified to a plain
`std::string` key list. Labels are derived from the key at render time. A dedicated config
struct can be introduced if per-unit labels or colors are needed in a future phase.

| Signal | Trigger |
|--------|---------|
| `unitsChanged()` | Any change to the unit list (add, remove, or batch) |
| `stateChanged()` (inherited) | Combined with `unitsChanged()` to trigger scene rebuild |

### 1.2 Unit Selection UI ✅

`HeatmapPropertiesWidget` now embeds a `Feature_Tree_Widget` (the same component used by
`DataViewer_Widget`) filtered to `DM_DataType::DigitalEvent` only:

- **Available keys** are shown in a tree, auto-grouped by prefix pattern
  (e.g. `unit_1`, `unit_2`, `unit_3` appear under a `unit` group node)
- **Checkbox per item** adds/removes that key from `HeatmapState::unit_keys`
- **Group checkbox** adds/removes all keys with the same prefix in a single click
- **Live updates** — the tree re-populates automatically when DataManager changes
  (via the DataManager observer; the observer is properly unregistered on widget destruction)
- **State sync** — on construction, existing `unit_keys` from serialized state are
  reflected in the tree checkboxes

This replaces the combo box + Add/Remove/Add-All button design described in the original
roadmap; the tree gives equivalent functionality with less UI surface and more visual
structure when many units are present.

### 1.3 Rate Estimation ⬜

For each unit, compute a rate profile using the same pipeline as [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd):

1. Call `WhiskerToolbox::Plots::createAlignedGatherResult<DigitalEventSeries>()` with the unit's key and the alignment settings
2. Bin aligned events into uniform time bins (same logic as `PSTHPlotOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()`)
3. Store the resulting counts as a row in the `units × time_bins` matrix

Initially, use raw counts divided by trial count (mean count per trial). More sophisticated
normalization is addressed in [Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization).

The rate estimation computation should be factored into a shared function as proposed in
the [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 2:

```cpp
namespace CorePlotting::RateEstimation {
    std::vector<double> estimateRate(
        GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries> const & gathered,
        RateEstimationMethod method,
        double bin_size,
        double window_size,
        double kernel_bandwidth = 20.0);
}
```

### 1.4 Heatmap Scene Building ⬜

Convert the rate matrix to a `RenderableRectangleBatch`:

- Each cell is one rectangle: X from `bin_start` to `bin_start + bin_width`, Y from `unit_index` to `unit_index + 1`
- Cell color is determined by mapping the rate value through a colormap (see [Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure))
- The batch is uploaded to `SceneRenderer` for rendering

### 1.5 Y-Axis Auto-fit ⬜

After computing the rate matrix, set Y bounds to `[0, num_units]` and configure the vertical
axis with `CorePlotting::trialIndexAxis(num_units)` mapping for integer unit labels.

---

## Phase 2: Colormap Infrastructure

**Status**: Not started. This is new shared infrastructure needed by both HeatmapWidget and
[SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd).

**Goal**: Provide a reusable colormap system for mapping scalar values to RGBA colors.

### 2.1 Colormap Types

```cpp
namespace CorePlotting::Colormaps {

/// A colormap is a function from [0, 1] → RGBA
using ColormapFunction = std::function<glm::vec4(float t)>;

/// Built-in colormaps
enum class ColormapPreset {
    Inferno,        ///< Default — perceptually uniform, dark-to-bright (matplotlib inferno)
    Viridis,        ///< Perceptually uniform, blue-green-yellow
    Magma,          ///< Perceptually uniform, dark-to-pink
    Plasma,         ///< Perceptually uniform, blue-to-yellow
    Coolwarm,       ///< Diverging blue-white-red (good for z-scores)
    GrayScale,      ///< Simple grayscale
    Hot             ///< Black-red-yellow-white
};

/// Get a colormap function by preset
ColormapFunction getColormap(ColormapPreset preset);

/// Map a scalar value through a colormap with configurable range
glm::vec4 mapValue(ColormapFunction const & cmap, float value, float vmin, float vmax);

/// Map an entire matrix to an RGBA image
std::vector<glm::vec4> mapMatrix(
    ColormapFunction const & cmap,
    std::vector<double> const & values,
    float vmin, float vmax);

}  // namespace CorePlotting::Colormaps
```

### 2.2 Location

Place colormap utilities in `src/CorePlotting/Colormaps/`. This library has no Qt dependency
and can be used by any rendering backend:

```
CorePlotting/Colormaps/
├── CMakeLists.txt
├── Colormap.hpp            # ColormapFunction, ColormapPreset, mapValue/mapMatrix
├── Colormap.cpp
├── InfernoData.hpp         # LUT data for Inferno (256 entries)
├── ViridisData.hpp         # LUT data for Viridis
└── ...
```

### 2.3 Shared Consumers

| Widget | Usage |
|--------|-------|
| **HeatmapWidget** | Rate matrix → colored heatmap cells |
| **[SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd)** | Power spectrum matrix → colored spectrogram cells |
| **[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd)** | External feature coloring ([EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 5) |

### 2.4 Default Colormap

The HeatmapWidget should default to **Inferno**, which is perceptually uniform and accessible
for color vision deficiency. When z-score scaling is active (see [Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)),
automatically suggest **Coolwarm** (diverging around zero).

---

## Phase 3: Scaling and Normalization

**Status**: Not started. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix).

**Goal**: Support multiple Y-value (color intensity) representations for the heatmap cells.

### 3.1 Scaling Enum

```cpp
enum class HeatmapScaling {
    FiringRate,     ///< Count / (bin_size_s × num_trials) → spikes/s (default)
    ZScore,         ///< Per-unit z-score: (rate - mean) / std across all bins
    Normalized01,   ///< Per-unit min-max normalization to [0, 1]
    RawCount,       ///< Raw event count (no normalization)
    CountPerTrial   ///< Count / num_trials
};
```

### 3.2 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    HeatmapScaling scaling = HeatmapScaling::FiringRate;
};
```

### 3.3 Implementation

After computing the raw count matrix in `rebuildScene()`:

```cpp
switch (scaling) {
    case HeatmapScaling::FiringRate:
        // rate = count / (bin_size_seconds * num_trials)
        break;
    case HeatmapScaling::ZScore:
        // Per-unit: z = (value - unit_mean) / unit_std
        // Best paired with Coolwarm colormap
        break;
    case HeatmapScaling::Normalized01:
        // Per-unit: v = (value - unit_min) / (unit_max - unit_min)
        break;
    case HeatmapScaling::CountPerTrial:
        // rate = count / num_trials
        break;
    case HeatmapScaling::RawCount:
        break;  // No transformation
}
```

### 3.4 Color Range Controls

When not using per-unit normalization (z-score, 0-1), the colormap needs a global value range:

- **Auto**: Determine range from data min/max (with optional percentile clipping)
- **Manual**: User-specified `vmin`/`vmax` spinboxes
- **Symmetric**: Force symmetric range around zero (useful for z-score)

```cpp
struct HeatmapColorRange {
    bool auto_range = true;
    double vmin = 0.0;
    double vmax = 1.0;
    bool symmetric = false;       ///< Force range to [-max(|vmin|,|vmax|), max(|vmin|,|vmax|)]
    double clip_percentile = 0.0; ///< Clip outliers at this percentile (0 = no clipping)
};
```

### 3.5 Properties Panel

Add a "Scaling" section to `HeatmapPropertiesWidget`:

- Scaling mode combo box
- Color range mode (Auto / Manual / Symmetric)
- `vmin` / `vmax` spinboxes (enabled when Manual)
- Clip percentile spinbox (enabled when Auto)

---

## Phase 4: Colorbar Display

**Status**: Not started. Depends on [Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure) and [Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization).

**Goal**: Render a color legend alongside the heatmap showing the value-to-color mapping.

### 4.1 ColorbarWidget

Create a lightweight `QWidget` that renders a vertical color gradient with tick labels:

```
CorePlotting/Widgets/
├── ColorbarWidget.hpp
└── ColorbarWidget.cpp
```

The `ColorbarWidget` needs:

- A `ColormapFunction` to render the gradient
- A value range (`vmin`, `vmax`) for tick label computation
- An axis label (e.g., "Firing Rate (Hz)", "Z-Score")
- Optional tick formatting (integer, decimal, scientific notation)

### 4.2 Integration with HeatmapWidget

Place the colorbar to the right of the OpenGL canvas in the `HeatmapWidget` layout:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┬────┐
│                                  │    │
│       HeatmapOpenGLWidget        │ CB │
│                                  │    │
├──────────────────────────────────┴────┤
│         RelativeTimeAxisWidget        │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The colorbar updates when the colormap, scaling mode, or value range changes.

### 4.3 Shared Consumer

The `ColorbarWidget` should be reusable by [SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd),
which also needs a color legend for its power spectrum display.

---

## Phase 5: Shared Rate Estimation Options

**Status**: Not started. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix).
Closely related to [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 2.

**Goal**: Share bin size, smoothing method, and rate estimation parameters between HeatmapWidget
and [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd).

### 5.1 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    double bin_size = 10.0;                                       ///< Bin size in time units
    RateEstimationMethod rate_method = RateEstimationMethod::Binning;
    double kernel_bandwidth = 20.0;                               ///< For kernel smoothing
};
```

### 5.2 Smoothing Methods

The same methods planned for [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) ([PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 2):

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `Binning` | Simple histogram binning (default) |
| `GaussianKernel` | Convolve binned counts with Gaussian kernel |
| `CausalExponential` | Causal exponential filter |
| `AdaptiveKernel` | Shimazaki & Shinomoto (2010) variable-bandwidth kernel |

For the heatmap, kernel smoothing is applied independently per unit (each row is smoothed
separately). The kernel bandwidth parameter controls the temporal resolution of the rate
estimate across all units.

### 5.3 Properties Panel

Add a "Rate Estimation" collapsible section to `HeatmapPropertiesWidget`:

- Bin size spinbox
- Rate estimation method combo box
- Kernel bandwidth spinbox (enabled for kernel methods)

These controls mirror the corresponding controls in `PSTHPropertiesWidget`, and should ideally
use shared UI components to avoid duplication.

---

## Phase 6: Row Sorting

**Status**: Not started. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix).

**Goal**: Allow users to reorder heatmap rows by various criteria for pattern discovery.

### 6.1 Sorting Modes

```cpp
enum class HeatmapSortMode {
    Manual,         ///< User-defined order (default — order of addition)
    TimeToPeak,     ///< Sort by latency of peak rate relative to t=0 (ascending)
    PeakRate,       ///< Sort by maximum rate value (descending)
    MeanRate,       ///< Sort by mean rate across all bins (descending)
    Alphabetical    ///< Sort by unit label / event key (ascending)
};
```

### 6.2 Time-to-Peak Sorting

The primary sorting mode. For each unit row:

1. Find the bin with the maximum rate value
2. The bin's center time relative to t = 0 is the "time to peak"
3. Sort units by time to peak in ascending order

This produces the characteristic "diagonal stripe" pattern seen in neuroscience publications,
revealing temporal sequences in population activity.

### 6.3 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    HeatmapSortMode sort_mode = HeatmapSortMode::Manual;
    bool sort_ascending = true;
};
```

### 6.4 Implementation

Sorting is applied after rate matrix computation and before colormap mapping:

```cpp
auto sort_indices = computeSortOrder(rate_matrix, sort_mode, sort_ascending);
auto sorted_matrix = reorderRows(rate_matrix, sort_indices);
auto sorted_units = reorderVector(units, sort_indices);
```

The sorted order should also be reflected in the Y-axis labels.

### 6.5 Properties Panel

Add a "Sorting" combo box to the properties panel with the available modes. Include an
ascending/descending toggle button.

---

## Phase 7: Cross-Widget Linking

**Status**: Design complete. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix).
Related to [EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 6 and
[PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 4.

**Goal**: Clicking a heatmap row updates [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) and
[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) to show the selected unit's data.

### 7.1 SelectionContext Integration

Following the existing `SelectionContext` pattern used by DataInspector widgets:

```cpp
// When user clicks a heatmap row:
void HeatmapOpenGLWidget::mouseReleaseEvent(QMouseEvent * event) {
    if (!_is_panning) {
        int unit_index = worldToUnitIndex(screenToWorld(event->pos()));
        auto const & unit_config = _state->units().at(unit_index);
        // Emit via SelectionContext:
        SelectionContext::instance().setSelectedData(
            unit_config.event_key, SelectionSource::HeatmapWidget);
    }
}
```

### 7.2 Receiving Widgets

In [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) and [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd)
(if not pinned):

```cpp
void PSTHPropertiesWidget::_onSelectionChanged(SelectionSource const & source) {
    if (source.type != SelectionSourceType::HeatmapWidget) return;
    if (_state->isPinned()) return;
    auto key = source.data_key;
    auto type = _data_manager->getDataType(key);
    if (type == DataType::DigitalEventSeries) {
        _state->clearPlotEvents();
        _state->addPlotEvent(key, PSTHEventOptions{.event_key = key});
        // Scene rebuilds automatically via stateChanged()
    }
}
```

### 7.3 Row Click → Unit Selection Flow

The primary cross-widget workflow:

```
HeatmapWidget row click
        ↓
SelectionContext::setSelectedData("unit_42", source)
        ↓
PSTHWidget (if not pinned) receives selectionChanged
        ↓
Clears existing events, adds "unit_42" → rebuilds histogram
        ↓
EventPlotWidget (if not pinned) also receives selectionChanged
        ↓
Updates raster plot for "unit_42"
```

This enables rapid exploration of neural data: click through units in the heatmap while
the PSTH and raster plot update in sync. See [EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)
Phase 6 and [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 4 for the complementary
receiver implementations.

### 7.4 Visual Row Highlighting

When a row is selected (either by click or via incoming `SelectionContext`), highlight
the selected row with a border or background overlay to provide visual feedback.

### 7.5 Pin Button

Add a `pinned` field to `HeatmapStateData` and a pin/unpin toggle button to the properties
panel toolbar. When pinned, the widget ignores `SelectionContext` changes, preserving its
current view. The pinned state is serialized for workspace restoration.

---

## Phase 8: Tooltip with Stacked PSTH + Raster

**Status**: Design exploration. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix)
and [Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking).

**Goal**: When hovering over a heatmap row, display a tooltip containing a miniature stacked
PSTH histogram and raster plot for that unit.

### 8.1 Concept

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Heatmap                           │
│  unit_A  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│  unit_B  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│  unit_C  ████████▓▓▓▓████████████████████████████  ← hover
│  unit_D  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│                                                     │
│          ┌─────────────────────┐                    │
│          │  unit_C PSTH        │                    │
│          │  ▄▄ ██ ▄▄ ..       │                    │
│          │─────────────────────│                    │
│          │  unit_C Raster      │                    │
│          │  | || |  || |       │                    │
│          │  ||  | | |  ||      │                    │
│          └─────────────────────┘                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 8.2 Lightweight Rendering

The tooltip must render quickly without creating full widget instances. Two approaches:

**Option A: Pre-rendered QPixmap Cache**

When the scene rebuilds, pre-render PSTH and raster thumbnails for each unit into `QPixmap`
objects. The tooltip simply displays the cached pixmap. This has memory cost proportional to
the number of units but provides instant tooltips.

**Option B: On-Demand QPainter Rendering**

When the hover event fires, compute the PSTH bins and raster tick positions for the hovered
unit using the cached `GatherResult` data, and render directly with `QPainter` into a tooltip
widget. This avoids the memory cost but requires fast rendering.

**Recommendation**: Option B with the cached `GatherResult` data from the scene rebuild.
The binning computation is fast (already done for the heatmap), and `QPainter` rendering
of a simple histogram + tick marks is lightweight.

### 8.3 Tooltip Content

The tooltip should show:

1. **Header**: Unit label / event key
2. **PSTH subplot**: Bar or line histogram of the unit's rate profile (same data as the heatmap row)
3. **Raster subplot**: Tick marks for each trial's events (using the unit's `GatherResult`)
4. **Optional statistics**: Peak rate, time to peak, mean rate

### 8.4 Implementation Approach

- Use a custom `QWidget` subclass as the tooltip (not `QToolTip::showText()`)
- Position the tooltip near the cursor using `QCursor::pos()`
- Show/hide with a short delay timer (similar to `EventPlotOpenGLWidget`'s tooltip timer)
- Reuse the shared rate estimation function for the PSTH portion
- Reuse `RasterMapper` logic for the raster portion (a simplified version without full scene building)

This is related to [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 6 (Embeddable PSTH for Tooltips),
which proposes decoupling the PSTH computation and rendering from the full widget machinery.

---

## Phase 9: Colormap Selection UI

**Status**: Not started. Depends on [Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure).

**Goal**: Allow users to select and preview colormaps from the properties panel.

### 9.1 Colormap Combo Box

Add a combo box to `HeatmapPropertiesWidget` that lists available `ColormapPreset` values.
Each entry should include a small colored gradient preview (rendered as a `QIcon` using the
colormap's 256-entry LUT).

### 9.2 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    ColormapPreset colormap = ColormapPreset::Inferno;
};
```

### 9.3 Auto-Suggestion

When the user switches to z-score scaling, automatically suggest the `Coolwarm` diverging
colormap (show a prompt or auto-switch with undo capability).

---

## Phase 10: PNG Export

**Status**: Not started. Qt provides the underlying infrastructure via `QOpenGLWidget::grabFramebuffer()`.

**Goal**: Export the current heatmap view as a publication-quality PNG image.

### 10.1 Basic Grab

The simplest approach uses Qt's built-in framebuffer grab:

```cpp
void HeatmapWidget::exportPNG(QString const & path) {
    QImage image = _opengl_widget->grabFramebuffer();
    image.save(path, "PNG");
}
```

This captures exactly what is displayed, including the current zoom/pan state.

### 10.2 High-Resolution Export

For publication quality, render at a higher resolution than screen:

```cpp
void HeatmapWidget::exportPNG(QString const & path, int scale_factor) {
    // Resize OpenGL widget to scaled dimensions
    // Render at high resolution
    // Grab framebuffer
    // Restore original size
}
```

### 10.3 Composite Export with Axes and Colorbar

For a complete figure, composite the OpenGL framebuffer with axis labels and colorbar:

1. Render the heatmap at target resolution
2. Render axes using `QPainter` at matching resolution
3. Render colorbar using `QPainter`
4. Composite into a single `QImage`
5. Save as PNG

### 10.4 UI Integration

Add an "Export PNG..." action to:

- The properties panel (button or menu)
- A right-click context menu on the plot

The dialog should offer:

- File path selection
- Resolution scale factor (1x, 2x, 4x)
- Include axes checkbox
- Include colorbar checkbox

### 10.5 SVG Export (Future)

The [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 5 proposes shared SVG export infrastructure
for all plot widgets using `SceneRenderer`. The HeatmapWidget should adopt this when available,
as SVG provides resolution-independent output for publications.

---

## Phase 11: AnalogTimeSeries Heatmap Variant (Future)

**Status**: Early conceptual design. Separate from the DigitalEventSeries heatmap.

**Goal**: A sibling widget that creates heatmaps from `AnalogTimeSeries` data rather than
discrete events. This enables visualization of continuous signals like ΔF/F calcium imaging
traces across multiple cells, aligned to events.

### 11.1 Concept

Where the current HeatmapWidget computes rate from discrete events (analogous to stacking
[PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) rows), the AnalogTimeSeries variant would display raw
continuous signals (analogous to stacking [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) rows).

| Feature | Event Heatmap (current) | Analog Heatmap (future) |
|---------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Data source | `DigitalEventSeries` | `AnalogTimeSeries` |
| Per-unit computation | Binning + rate estimation | Direct value sampling / interpolation |
| Analogous single-unit view | [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) | [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) |
| Typical use case | Population spike rate | ΔF/F, LFP, EMG across units |
| Normalization | Firing rate, z-score | Z-score, ΔF/F₀, % change |

### 11.2 Shared Infrastructure

Despite the different data sources, significant infrastructure is shared:

- **Colormap system** ([Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure))
- **Colorbar widget** ([Phase 4](#phase-4-colorbar-display))
- **Sorting modes** ([Phase 6](#phase-6-row-sorting)) — time-to-peak, peak value, etc.
- **Cross-widget linking** ([Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking)) — click row → update linked widgets
- **PNG/SVG export** ([Phase 10](#phase-10-png-export))
- **Scaling/normalization** ([Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)) — z-score, 0-1 normalization

### 11.3 Different Infrastructure

Key differences that may warrant a separate widget rather than a mode switch:

- **Data gathering**: `GatherResult<AnalogTimeSeries>` instead of `GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries>`
- **Value computation**: Direct sampling/interpolation instead of binning + rate estimation
- **Alignment**: May align to `DigitalIntervalSeries` only (baseline period for ΔF/F₀ normalization)
- **Time resolution**: Native sampling rate rather than binned; may need downsampling for display
- **Tooltip**: Would show a line trace rather than a PSTH histogram

### 11.4 Recommendation

Implement as a **separate widget** (`AnalogHeatmapWidget`) that reuses the colormap,
colorbar, sorting, linking, and export infrastructure. The rendering and data gathering
paths are sufficiently different that forcing them into a single widget would add unwanted
complexity. The two widgets can coexist in the Plot menu, clearly labeled:

- **Plot → Heatmap Plot (Events)** — current widget
- **Plot → Heatmap Plot (Analog)** — future widget

---

## Implementation Priority

### Next Up (Core Functionality)

1. **[Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix)**: Unit selection + rate matrix + basic rendering
2. **[Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure)**: Colormap infrastructure (shared with SpectrogramWidget)
3. **[Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)**: Scaling/normalization modes

### Following (Polish and Features)

4. **[Phase 4](#phase-4-colorbar-display)**: Colorbar display
5. **[Phase 5](#phase-5-shared-rate-estimation-options)**: Shared rate estimation options (with PSTHWidget)
6. **[Phase 6](#phase-6-row-sorting)**: Row sorting (time-to-peak first)
7. **[Phase 9](#phase-9-colormap-selection-ui)**: Colormap selection UI

### Integration

8. **[Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking)**: Cross-widget linking
9. **[Phase 8](#phase-8-tooltip-with-stacked-psth--raster)**: Tooltip with stacked PSTH + raster

### Export and Extensions

10. **[Phase 10](#phase-10-png-export)**: PNG export
11. **[Phase 11](#phase-11-analogtimeseries-heatmap-variant-future)**: AnalogTimeSeries variant (separate widget)

### Open Questions

1. **Shared rate estimation extraction**: Should the rate estimation be factored out of PSTHWidget
   before or during HeatmapWidget Phase 1? Doing it first enables clean reuse; doing it during
   avoids premature abstraction.

2. **Colormap data format**: Should colormaps be stored as 256-entry LUTs (fast lookup, fixed
   resolution) or as piecewise-linear functions (arbitrary resolution, smaller storage)?

3. **Row label rendering**: Should unit labels be rendered as axis tick labels via `VerticalAxisWidget`,
   or as a separate label column? The axis approach reuses existing infrastructure but may not
   handle long labels well.

4. **Performance at scale**: What is the target unit count? 100 units × 200 bins = 20K rectangles
   is trivial for OpenGL. 1000 units × 1000 bins = 1M rectangles may need texture-based rendering
   instead of rectangle batches.

5. **AnalogTimeSeries variant timing**: Should the analog heatmap be a mode of this widget
   or a completely separate widget? The shared infrastructure is substantial, but the data
   pipelines are fundamentally different.

# See Also

- [HeatmapWidget Documentation](index.qmd) — Full architecture and feature documentation
- [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md) — Shared architecture, axis types, DataManager integration
- [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) — Single-unit PSTH; shares rate estimation pipeline
- [PSTHWidget Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Rate estimation factoring, embeddable PSTH for tooltips
- [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) — Trial-level raster plot; shares alignment and linking
- [EventPlotWidget Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Cross-widget linking, external feature coloring
- [SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd) — Shares colormap infrastructure
